tour_name: Places Parisians Love: Classic Treasures, Hidden Gems & Locals' Picks

Our guide was exceptionally knowledgeable and made the tour incredibly interesting and engaging. They shared a range of fascinating facts that went well beyond what you can find online. From quaint alleys to lesser-known historical and artistic sites, every stop was enriched by the guide’s insights and enthusiasm. Highly recommended for anyone looking to explore the unseen side of Paris! Definitely one of the best walking tours we’ve done.

tour_name: Le Marais without the crowds, the beating heart of Paris
review_text: Wir haben heute eine Führung durch das Marais gemacht und waren begeistert. Unser Guide sprach ausgezeichnetes Englisch, erklärte komplexe historische Zusammenhänge klar, lebendig und mit viel Charme und Humor. Besonders beeindruckt hat uns sein enormes Wissen: Er beantwortete jede Frage fundiert, verständlich und mit spannenden zusätzlichen Details. Die Geschichte des Marais wurde nicht einfach nur erzählt, sondern so vermittelt, dass Menschen, Ereignisse und Orte lebendig wurden und lange in Erinnerung bleiben. Die Tour war gleichermaßen informativ, unterhaltsam und inspirierend. Wer das Marais wirklich verstehen und nicht nur besichtigen möchte, ist hier genau richtig. Eine der besten Stadtführungen, die wir je erlebt haben. Uneingeschränkte Empfehlung.

tour_name: Paris : visite à pied guidée de Montmartre
review_text: Eine absolut großartige Führung durch Montmartre! Unsere Guide hat die Geschichte und die besonderen Geschichten dieses Viertels auf eine unglaublich lebendige und anschauliche Weise vermittelt. Man merkt sofort, mit wie viel Leidenschaft, Wissen und Begeisterung sie dabei ist. Besonders schön war, dass sie nicht einfach nur Fakten aufgezählt hat, sondern viele Hintergrundinformationen und Details erzählt hat, die man in keinem Reiseführer findet. Ihre freundliche, offene und enthusiastische Art hat die gesamte Tour zu einem echten Erlebnis gemacht. Der Höhepunkt war für mich ein kleiner Park hinter Montmartre, den ich alleine vermutlich niemals entdeckt hätte. Gerade solche versteckten Orte und die dazugehörigen Geschichten machen den Unterschied zwischen einer guten und einer außergewöhnlichen Führung aus. Wer Montmartre wirklich verstehen und erleben möchte, dem kann ich diese Tour uneingeschränkt empfehlen. Vielen Dank für dieses wunderbare Erlebnis!

review_text: Nema, our guide was well informed, articulate, good with people, and passionate about Paris. We loved following in the footsteps of the great artists who flooded to mommartre in the 19thC. We saw the mill restaurant where m Renoir produced one of his most famous paintings. We saw the restaurant and grocery where Amelie was filmed, an award winning bakery, the shops that make Montmartre so special. We found out why Paris was a country for just a few months. I would highly recommend this tour.

review_text: The tour was great! I do feel as if it was a little rushed. I appreciated the guide wanting to walk us through all of the markets and giving us the lay of the land - but it did make it feel awkward when you wanted to explore an area more but felt like you needed to move on. Would have preferred either a tour to each market with free time at each one, or a quicker walking tour through the whole market so we would have had energy at the end to go back to stalls/vendors - it felt too long and somehow also too rushed how it was set up. HOWEVER, we never would have been able to navigate to it or through it on our own. It was also very informative and insightful. All in all I would recommend this tour but would want folks to level set what sort of experience they might have!

review_text: I was really excited to go on that Latin Quarter tour, and I couldn't have gotten a better guide than Yago. He's young and dynamic, and very passionate about Paris. The stories about the jazz, the very catholic neighbourhood, all the academic institutions, the cinema in the district... I just want to move there! I was lucky to be in a small group with two very funny American people who made us listen to jazz. He also brought us into a church that was absolutely marvellous. He gave me some tips to enter Notre Dame when there's just a few people, and an extensive list of restaurants. I definitely recommend that tour and wish you'll meet Yago!

review_text: My visit to the Louvre that day had been cancelled because of the robbery! didn't happen since Mona Lisa disappeared in the 20th century... Despite this, I booked this tour in order to know more about the museum, and becuase I loved the Latin Quarter tour on Friday, with the same company. Philip is a very funny guide and gave us incredible stories about the monuments we saw: Notre Dame, Academie francaise, Conciergerie with Marie antoinette... Finally, we entered the court of the museum, and I'm very grateful he answered all my questions about Louvre's history after the tour. I'm almostt happy the museum was closed so I could hear his stories! And we were lcuky enough for the rain to pour only the last minutes of the tour. I highly recommend it! Thanks Philip

review_text: Flo was an incredibly experienced guide, having lived in Paris for over 40 years. She started off with the history of the city's creation and evolution, beginning with the construction of the Roman walls. The tour covered everything from ancient history to modern fashion brands and included all the iconic tourist spots and landmarks. For first-timers like me and my wife, it was a fantastic introduction to the city. Flo’s way of explaining things was both funny and engaging. She encouraged us to ask questions and was very knowledgeable, answering everything in detail. She even made us guess some answers, which made the tour interactive and fun. I’d definitely recommend it!

Seine äußerst sympathische und ruhige Art und weise ist in einer so hektischen Stadt echt angenehm. Immer wieder gerne !!!
